content.thinking: I can see the ray-head Service only has the GCS port (6379) exposed. I need to add port 8265 for the dashboard. Let me patch the service to add the dashboard port.

content.text: The service only has port 6379 (GCS). I'll patch it to add port 8265 for the dashboard.

input.description: Add dashboard port 8265 to ray-head Service

input.command: kubectl -n ray exec curl-test -- curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://ray-head:8265/api/cluster_status

input.description: Check listening ports on ray-head pod

content.thinking:
Let me decode these hex ports to see what's listening. The hex values are:
- CC8D = 52365
- F1AF = 61871
- F2F1 = 62193
- ACB9 = 44217
- ACC3 = 44227

None of those are 8265 (0x2049). Let me also check the IPv6 socket file and see if there are any other ports, and also check on what ports Ray is configured to run.

input.command: kubectl -n ray exec ray-head-5c586c8fd6-zb27n -- cat /proc/net/tcp6 2>/dev/null | head -20

content.thinking:
Let me decode the listening ports from tcp6:
- FAD0 = 64208
- 8AED = 35565
- 8F1B = 36635
- A5E1 = 42465
- 18EB = 6379 (GCS port!)
- 2711 = 10001

So port 8265 (0x2049) is NOT listening! The dashboard isn't running on the head pod. Let me check the Ray head container's startup command and see what's going on.

Actually, let me check if 8265 appears anywhere in the process list on the head pod.
Port 8265 is NOT in the list of listening ports. So the dashboard isn't running. Let me check what the Ray head pod is configured with and look at its startup args.

content.text: Port 8265 isn't listening at all. Let me check how Ray was started on the head pod.

input.command: kubectl -n ray get pod ray-head-5c586c8fd6-zb27n -o yaml | grep -A 30 'containers:'

content.content:
containers:
  - command:
    - sh
    - -c
    - |
      ray start --head --node-ip-address="${MY_POD_IP}" --port=6379 --include-dashboard=false --num-cpus=0 --block
    env:
    - name: MY_POD_IP
      valueFrom:
        fieldRef:
          apiVersion: v1
          fieldPath: status.podIP
    - name: RAY_NODE_ID
      valueFrom:
        fieldRef:
          apiVersion: v1
          fieldPath: metadata.uid
    - name: RAY_RAYLET_PID
      value: "1"
    image: rayproject/ray:2.9.0
    imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
    name: ray-head
    ports:
    - containerPort: 6379
      name: gcs
      protocol: TCP
    resources: {}
    terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
    terminationMessagePolicy: File
    volumeMounts:
    - mountPath: /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount

content.thinking:
The Ray head is started with `--include-dashboard=false`. This is why the dashboard isn't running. I need to:
1. Update the deployment to start Ray with `--include-dashboard=true` (and also `--dashboard-host=0.0.0.0` to make it accessible from outside the pod) and add port 8265 to the container ports.
2. The Service already has port 8265 now (I patched it).

Let me get the Deployment resource and patch it.

content.text: The head starts with `--include-dashboard=false`. I need to fix the Deployment to enable it and add the container port.

input.command: kubectl -n ray get deployment ray-head -o yaml | grep -A 5 'containers:'
